objective: Default to the vLLM nightly install path for Qwen 3.5 support while preserving a reversible stable fallback, and reduce noisy optional environment warnings in RunPod test configuration.
scope: Apply minimal fork-local runtime/config updates in Dockerfile, .runpod/tests.json, and aiwf.request.json only: set VLLM_NIGHTLY default true with coherent single-path install logic, define explicit optional env defaults used by RunPod tests (without HF_TOKEN), and document compatibility/rollback/deploy safeguards for RunPod + vLLM.
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile defaults VLLM_NIGHTLY to true and keeps existing CUDA 12.8 runtime baseline unchanged.
Dockerfile uses a single coherent install branch (nightly or stable) to avoid contradictory dual-install behavior during image builds.

.runpod/tests.json config.env includes explicit defaults for HEALTH_CHECK_INIT_FAST_MODE, HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS, SCALING_MIN_QUEUE_TIME_MS, SCALING_THRESHOLD_BUFFER_MS, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT, POSTHOG_API_KEY, RUNPOD_GQL_API_URL, and GIN_MODE.
.runpod/tests.json does not introduce HF_TOKEN or any new secret requirement.
All edits remain fork-local, minimal, and reversible via documented rollback actions.
tests_expected: Run a fork-local RunPod test job to verify optional env warning noise is reduced without requiring HF_TOKEN
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback: set Dockerfile ARG VLLM_NIGHTLY back to false and restore prior two-step install flow if nightly regressions appear; remove newly added optional env defaults from .runpod/tests.json to return to prior behavior; revert this change set in one fork-local commit to restore previous runtime/test configuration. No upstream operations required.
deploy_plan: Build and validate in this fork only. Run JSON validation and docker buildx bake print checks before merge, then run a fork-local RunPod smoke test using the existing test suite to verify startup and inference behavior under the nightly default path.
threat_model: Key risks are nightly package instability, dependency drift from pre-release wheels, and subtle runtime behavior changes from explicit env defaults. Mitigations: keep CUDA base image unchanged, preserve a stable fallback branch controlled by VLLM_NIGHTLY, avoid introducing new secrets (no HF_TOKEN), and require fast fork-local rollback through a minimal three-file revert.
